Actually, let me reconsider - the proper approach is to aggregate each channel separately then combine:

```json
{
  "action": "sql",
  "sql": "SELECT ca_state, year, SUM(revenue) AS combined_revenue FROM (SELECT ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date) AS year, SUM(cs.cs_net_paid_inc_tax) AS revenue FROM catalog_sales cs LEFT JOIN customer_address ca ON cs.cs_bill_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk LEFT JOIN date_dim d ON cs.cs_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk GROUP BY ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date) UNION ALL SELECT ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date) AS year, SUM(ss.ss_net_paid_inc_tax) AS revenue FROM store_sales ss LEFT JOIN customer_address ca ON ss.ss_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk LEFT JOIN date_dim d ON ss.ss_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk GROUP BY ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date) UNION ALL SELECT ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date) AS year, SUM(ws.ws_net_paid_inc_tax) AS revenue FROM web_sales ws LEFT JOIN customer_address ca ON ws.ws_bill_addr_sk = ca.ca_address_sk LEFT JOIN date_dim d ON ws.ws_sold_date_sk = d.d_date_sk GROUP BY ca.ca_state, EXTRACT(YEAR FROM d.d_date)) GROUP BY ca_state, year ORDER BY ca_state, year;",
  "reason": "Each UNION branch must have GROUP BY. Outer query aggregates combined results by state and year."
}
```
